data.body:
Pass-1 review — T3 (Integration feasibility) [overlay]: PASS / not-applicable. No findings.

The overlay applies only when a plan integrates an external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a never-before-used capability. This plan does neither — every integration it touches is internal and already in active, tested use:

- Token/model sizing helpers it reuses all exist in-repo: `largest_window_tokens` + `models_at_or_above` (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sizing.py), `est_tokens` (det_floor.py), `resolve_model` (src/rebar/llm/config.py).
- The visual editor's external libs (`bpmn-moddle`, `bpmn-auto-layout`, `bpmn-js-properties-panel`) are vendored and covered by a faithful E2E harness (tests/e2e/) plus unit round-trip tests (tests/unit/workflow/test_bpmn.py, test_editor.py).
- WS2's "per-step `model:` round-trips through BPMN" rides the EXISTING `<rebar:Config>` extension mechanism: `_config_json` (src/rebar/llm/workflow/bpmn.py:226) serializes every non-`_STRUCTURAL` step key, and `model` is not in `_STRUCTURAL` (bpmn.py:100) — so it round-trips by construction. This is internal serializer behaviour, not a new external capability.

No external subcommand/endpoint is asserted (no MISMATCH risk); no new auth/HTTPS surface; no critical capability gap that would require a SPIKE. T3 raises nothing.
